title: astragalus echinus subsp. echinus
intro: Astragalus echinus subsp. echinus, commonly known as the spiny milk-vetch, is a perennial herb belonging to the legume family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Astragalus echinus subsp. echinus include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of various diseases, such as arthritis and respiratory infections. Astragalus echinus subsp. echinus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
